headline: Reset and reopen
focus: small caps and new annual themes
analysis: Capital rotates back after year-end positioning, but liquidity is still selective before the long holiday.
strategy: Build starter positions and keep room for volatility around the holiday break.

headline: Spring rally window
analysis: This is usually the cleanest risk-on window of Q1. Fast money and institutions both lean into new annual narratives.
strategy: Trade offensively, but stay with liquid leaders instead of chasing thin tails.

headline: Policy-theme expansion
analysis: Headline policy language becomes the main catalyst, so narrative strength matters as much as pure earnings.
strategy: Trade the confirmed policy lanes and avoid overextended secondary copies.

headline: Earnings stress test
analysis: The market stops rewarding stories without numbers. Report season usually punishes weak balance sheets and crowded momentum trades.
strategy: Cut weak themes quickly and rotate toward businesses with cleaner visibility.

headline: Lower-conviction tape
analysis: Seasonality usually turns more selective. Rotation gets slower and broad participation weakens.
strategy: Run lighter exposure and favor names that can hold without constant flow support.

headline: Mid-year liquidity squeeze
focus: large caps and cyclicals with valuation support
analysis: Risk appetite often fades into the half-year turn, especially when institutions prioritize balance-sheet optics.
strategy: Trade smaller and wait for cleaner setups instead of forcing trend continuation.

headline: Interim results preview
analysis: The market starts splitting between names with visible interim growth and themes still running on narrative only.
strategy: Anchor on earnings revisions and be ready for fast style rotation.

headline: Results digestion
analysis: Report season keeps the tape fragmented. Good numbers help, but misses are punished quickly.
strategy: Protect capital first and only add where results improve forward expectations.

headline: Choppy transition month
analysis: There is often no dominant market-wide trend. Traders get paid more by timing than by sitting in passive exposure.
strategy: Trade tactically, keep stops tight, and avoid assuming every bounce becomes a trend.

headline: Repricing after reports
focus: quality leaders with cleaner Q3 visibility
analysis: Once the holiday passes, the market often starts rebuilding conviction around names that survived report season cleanly.
strategy: Accumulate quality where guidance and price action start lining up again.

headline: Institutional rotation month
focus: undervalued cyclicals and new leadership candidates
analysis: Funds start leaning into the next annual playbook. This can produce powerful rotation away from crowded winners.
strategy: Follow fresh sponsorship and do not cling to tired leaders from the prior run.

headline: Ranking defense and reset
analysis: Early December often stays defensive while funds protect rankings. Late in the month, the market starts probing next-year candidates.
strategy: Stay patient early, then build watchlists aggressively for the first quarter reopen.
